EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms with very heavy rain caused flash flooding across central and southern Yavapai County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The intersection of 6th and Cyprus Roads was flooded. A spotter recorded 0.75 inches of rain in 30 minutes near the intersection.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms with very heavy rain caused flash flooding across central and southern Yavapai County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flash flooding was reported on State Highway 169 near Yarber Wash and a section of the highway was closed.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Lightning stuck and killed a man from the town of Nazlini.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 21-year-old man from the town of Nazlini was watching a rain swollen waterfall from a metal bridge. He was struck by a lightning bolt while standing on the bridge. He was transported to a Phoenix hospital where he later died.  Other individuals in the vicinity of the strike were knocked over by the shock wave but were not seriously injured.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain caused a rock slide southeast of Bagdad.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain from a thunderstorm caused a rock slide that closed State Route 95 at Mile Post 4 southeast of Bagdad.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain caused flash flooding in St Johns.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain from thunderstorms caused flooding in one home flooded and  threatened another home.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A funnel cloud was sighted from Sunset Crater.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A funnel cloud was sighted from Sunset Crater National Monument northeast of Flagstaff.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorm produced nickel sized hail in Doney Park.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorm produced nickel sized hail (7/8 inch diameter) hail was reported in Doney Park.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain from a thunderstorm caused a flash flood in Antelope Canyon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Five feet of water was flowing in the normally dry Antelope Canyon. This slot canyon is about 5 miles east of Page.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain caused a vehicle to hydroplane and crash on I-17.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain caused a vehicle to hydroplane and crash.  One person was killed.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorm in Bellemont produced severe wind gusts.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorm produced wind gusts to 60 MPH at the NWS office in Bellemont.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A dust devil in Tonto Village caused damage to a carport.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A dust devil caused structure damage to a carport in Tonto Village.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm wind gusts in Yavapai County caused damage.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A storage shed was blown over in a dry microbust about one mile east of Prescott Valley.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain from a thunderstorm caused Cebecue Creek in Cebecue to flood.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain from a thunderstorm caused flooding in Cebecue and along Indian Route 12 to Highway 60. The flood water reached the bedroom windows in at least one house in Cebecue.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstoms produced very heavy rain in Chino Valley.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flood water was flowing over Reed Road in northwest Chino Valley.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms produced heavy rain in the Williams area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain from thunderstorms caused flooding in the Williams area. Two feet of water covered Country Club Road at the railroad underpass.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorm produced a funnel cloud northeast of St Johns.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A funnel cloud dropped out of the base of a cloud then went back up.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A severe thunderstorm produced torrential rain, hail, and flooding in and around Lake Montezuma.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Nickle sized hail was reported in Lake Montezuma.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorm caused flash flooding west of Dilkon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flash flooding brought high water over route 15 between mile markers 59 and 63 about 5 miles west of Dilkon.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong thunderstorms with very heavy rain caused flash flooding in central Apache County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Numerous road closures due to highwater and mud between St. Johns and Springerville,  between St. Johns and Hunt,  between St. Johns and Concho,  and between Concho and Hunt.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain from a thunderstorm caused flash flooding on the south of Sedona.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain from a thunderstorm caused flooding in the Sedona area. Mud, boulders, and high water blocked Highway 179 south side of Sedona. Some vehicles were stalled in the high water.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorm brought torrential rain and strong winds to Peeples Valley.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm winds removed tree branches 1 to 2 inches in diameter from trees.  The thunderstorm also brought torrential rain.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms caused flash flooding in central Apache County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain from thunderstorms caused flash flooding along Highway 191 betwee Sanders and St Johns.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain from thunderstorms caused flash flooding west of the town of Congress.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A rancher reported flash flooding along Date Creek 4 miles east of Highway  93 near several homes. The creek flowed out of its banks and over a dike on the ranch.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Runoff from heavy rain caused several road closures in Williams.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Several streets were closed in Williams due to water in streets from heavy rain.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain caused flash flooding near Kaibito.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Highway 98 was closed due to flooding near mile marker 330.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Flash flooding washed out a county road southeast of Woodruff.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flash flooding washed out County road 9332.  A 36 inch culvert was washed downstream and a 4 to 5 foot deep gully was scoured in the roadbed.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain over the Brins fire scar caused a rock slide over Highway 89 in Oak Creek Canyon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Sedona Fire Department reported small rocks (softball-sized) and one large  boulder fell on Highway 89 in Oak Creek Canyon. This happened after heavy rain fell over the Brins fire scar.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain from a thunderstorm caused flash flooding near Dilkon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flash flooding was reported along Navajo Route 15 near mile post 69. Flooding was also reported on Indian Route 6 between White Cone and Indian Wells at mile post 22.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain caused a rock slide on Highway 89 south of Prescott.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain caused a rock slide on Highway 89 (mile marker 305) south of Prescott.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain from a thunderstorm caused extensive flooding to parts of the Northern Arizona University Campus.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain caused flooding across the southeast parts of the Northern Arizona University.  Water entered several buildings and flooded parking lots with close to three feet of water.  An electric substation was flooded and caused a power outage.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain caused flash flooding in Chino Valley.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Numerous city streets flooded in Chino Valley and high water flowing across many low water crossings.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Wind from a thunderstorm blew down a tree in Washington Park.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm winds blew down a tree onto power lines in Washington Park.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The Birdie Fire was started by lightning on July 6th about 4 miles southwes 0f Mormon Lake Village.  Over 650 people were able to contain the fire at about 5,000 acres by July 14th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Birdie Fire was lightning caused on July 6th and was contained at about 5000 acres on July 14th. The fire was approximately 4 miles southwest of Mormon Lake Village in dense ponderosa pine and rugged, rocky terrain. Over 650 personnel, 16 crews, 20 engines, 5 dozers, 10 water tenders, and five helicopters took part in the suppression efforts.|| |

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Wind gusts from a thunderstorm caused damage in Winslow.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Winds from a thunderstorm snapped power poles in and around Winslow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain caused flash flooding north of Ash Fork.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain from a thunderstorm caused flash flooding about 7 miles north of Ash Fork. The flooding covered roadways.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorm produced quarter sized (1 inch in diameter) in Camp Verde.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Quarter sized (1 inch in diameter) fell in Camp Verde.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Severe hail was reported with a thunderstorm in Flagstaff.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorm produced hail upto 3/4 inch in diamiter.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A severe thunderstorm produced one inch diameter hail in Camp Verde.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A severe thunderstorm produced 1 inch diameter hail in Camp Verde.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorm outflow caused a dust storm to reduce the visability to 1/4 mile six miles north of Snowflake.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A severe thunderstorm produced one inch diameter hail in Washington Park.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorm produced 1 inch diameter hail in Washington Park.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A severe thunderstorm produced hail near Prescott.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorm produced 3/4 inch hail about 6 miles north-northwest of Prescott.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms caused flash flooding in the Linden area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flash flooding covered several roads with water about one mile south of Linden.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain caused flooding in downtown Flagstaff.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flash flooding covered the intersection of Cottage and Leroux streets near downtown Flagstaff with three feet of water making the road impassable.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms with very heavy rain caused flash flooding across central and southern Yavapai County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Several homes flooded from Verde Village to Cottonwood.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms with very heavy rain caused flash flooding across central and southern Yavapai County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flash flooding on UVX Road and Bates Road. The roads closed because they were covered with 1 to 1 1/2 feet of water.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A severe thunderstorm produced one inch diameter hail about 5 miles north of Ash Fork.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A severe thunderstorm produced 1 inch diameter hail about 5 miles north of Ash Fork for close to ten minutes.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorm produced very heavy rainfall south of Holbrook.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Very heavy rain caused flooding along and over Highway 377 about 10 miles south-southwest of Holbrook. The water knocked over barged wire right of way fencing. Cars were backed up along the highway because of the flooding.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Subtropical moisture over the Desert Southwest fueled thunderstorms which brought heavy rain and flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Several roads were closed and/or damaged by flooding, including U.S. Highway 93 20 miles north of Wikieup, Stockton Hills Rd. in the Kingman area, and Antares Rd. and Diamond Bar Rd. north of Kingman.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Monsoon moisture fueled late-season thunderstorms.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Blowing dust caused a ten-car pileup on Interstate 40 near Yucca.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Monsoon moisture fueled late-season thunderstorms.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning set fire to a drug store.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper level low stalled west of Baja for several days bringing northward ample subtropical moisture. On November 30th the upper low pushed east toward Arizona brining heavy rainfall and strong winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A measured gust to 123mph was reported at Kitt Peak. No damage was reported.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Ample monsoonal moisture combined with daytime heating caused strong thunderstorms over Santa Cruz County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A severe thunderstorm over the city of Nogales caused hail one inch in diameter.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong low pressure system brought thunderstorms and heavy rain to the Mojave Desert.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Diamond Bar Rd. was closed and washes were flooded in Meadview.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A moist easterly flow due to high pressure over the four corners combined with daytime heating to cause severe thunderstorms with strong winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Approximately 100 trees were uprooted and knocked down at Oro Valley Country Club on Greenock Road due to a wet microburst.  An additional 30 trees were uprooted at a nearby shopping plaza at Oracle Road and 1st Avenue.  The uprooted trees caused roof damage to several houses.  The storms also blew off part of a roof at the Blue Moon stables in Oro Valley.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Abundant monsoonal moisture caused numerous thunderstorms across much of Southeast Arizona.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorm near Safford produced a strong outflow resulting in a dust storm. The visibility dropped to about 100 feet.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Abundant monsoonal moisture caused numerous thunderstorms across much of Southeast Arizona.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Outflow from thunderstorms in the Tucson area pushed northwest into Southeast Pinal County causing reduced visibilities due to blowing dust.  Visibilities were near zero along Interstate 10 between Red Rock and Eloy causing a traffic accident near Interstate 10 and Arizona 347.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Abundant monsoonal moisture caused numerous thunderstorms across much of Southeast Arizona.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A large cottonwood tree was downed across State Highway 80 due to thunderstorms.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Abundant monsoonal moisture caused numerous thunderstorms across much of Southeast Arizona.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained spotter estimated one inch hail near Arivaca Junction.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Abundant monsoonal moisture caused numerous thunderstorms across much of Southeast Arizona.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: An estimated gust to 60 mph was observed at the NWS office on the University of Arizona.  There was a recorded gust to 69 mph on the roof of the Atmospheric Sciences building.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Daytime heating in combination with a moist flow caused thunderstorms to develop across Southeast Arizona.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flooding and debris occurred along 8th street in Safford due to Flash Flooding.  In addition, Klondyke Wash west of Safford was impassible.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Daytime heating in combination with a moist flow caused thunderstorms to develop across Southeast Arizona.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flash flooding occurred in Wards Canyon in Clifton. 2 feet of mud and rock was mucked out from under the Wards Canyon bridge.  In addition, two vehicles were stuck in soapbox wash.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The first strong cold front of the season brought strong southwest winds ahead of it, and strong north winds behind it.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Occurred 4 miles ENE of Bullhead City, AZ.  Two wooden barns and one metal shed were blown over, and there was light to moderate damage to several shingled roofs.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Daytime heating in combination with a moist flow caused thunderstorms to develop across Southeast Arizona.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Two people were rescued from rising floodwater on Border road west of Douglas after their car overheated.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong thunderstorms caused high winds and excessive rainfall throughout the Tucson Metro area and a dust storm in Pinal County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Microburst near Tangerine and Interstate 10 in the Gladden Farms neighborhood caused some tree and roof damage.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong thunderstorms caused high winds and excessive rainfall throughout the Tucson Metro area and a dust storm in Pinal County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Localized areas in the midtown of Tucson had over one inch of rainfall causing flash flooding. A car had been swept nearly 100 yards down Rodeo Wash east of North Avenue and West Irvington Road. One stalled car was trapped under several feet of water in the Stone Avenue underpass near downtown.  There was also a swift water rescue near Kleindale and Tucson Boulevard.  At Fort Lowell Road and North Mountain Avenue several vehicles were trapped in flooding washes.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong thunderstorms caused high winds and excessive rainfall throughout the Tucson Metro area and a dust storm in Pinal County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A dust storm with visibility near zero caused a three vehicle collision on Interstate 10 near Picacho Peak.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Monsoonal moisture caused thunderstorms to develop across the White Mountains in Northern Greenlee County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Hail 3/4 inch in diameter was reported in Hannagan Meadow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong and slow moving thunderstorms with heavy rain and strong winds moved through portions of the Tucson area causing flash flooding and wind damage.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Several trees down near Broadway and Kolb.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong and slow moving thunderstorms with heavy rain and strong winds moved through portions of the Tucson area causing flash flooding and wind damage.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong winds from a thunderstorm knocked down about 20 large power poles along North 11th Avenue south of Speedway Blvd. in Tucson.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong weather system brought localized very strong winds to Doney Park.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Very strong wind was reported in Doney Park during the day. Sustained wind of 30 to 40 MPH with gusts to 55 MPH lasted for several hours during the day. A fire station in the area reported a peak wind gust of 82 MPH. The very strong wind was not a widespread event.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A winter storm brought heavy snow over the southern portions of the Western Mogollon Rim.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy snow fell along near the edge of the Mogollon Rim.  Spotters in Munds Park reported 6 to 8 inches of snow in 5 hours.  Nine inches of snow fell in Mormon Lake.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong winter storm produced heavy snow over the Kaibab Plateau.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A strong winter storm produced heavy snow with near white out conditons at times over the Kaibab Plateau. Eighteen inches of new snow fell at Jacob Lake and 22 inches of new snow fell at the North Rim Entrance.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: There were several reports of dense fog early this morning. The previous days rainfall combined with cool temperatures and resulted in visibility less than a quarter mile.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: At one point the visibility was limited to about 200 feet in the Coolidge-Florence area.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rainfall throughout Yuma was related to outages affecting about 1000 customers.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: About 1000 APS customers were left without power after heavy rains triggered fires to equipment on 15 power poles.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The Promontory Fire started on Sunday May 13 along the Mogollon Rim about 15 miles northeast of Payson. The fire then fell into See Canyon with steep (cliffs in some places) drop offs. The top of the fire was above 7800 feet and the lower part of the fire was near 6000 feet.  ||An outflow gust front from a thunderstorm hit the fire down on May 16. This pushed the fire toward summer homes in See Canyon Estates and Mountain Meadows which were evacuated.  The fire burned a total of  4044 acres but, no structures lost.

A strong area of low pressure from the Pacific Northwest brought heavy rain, very windy conditions, and high elevation snow on Friday December 7th through Saturday December 8th. The next portion of the storm system began to move across the state on late Sunday December 9th and lasted through Tuesday December 11th. This brought heavy snow to many areas along and north of the Mogollon Rim and the White Mountains.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain fell across southern Coconino County from December 7th though the  11th. The snow level gradually lowered with the first part of the storm. The second half of the storm brought rapidly lowering snow levels. Rain and melted snow amounts ranged from around 2 inches in Flagstaff and Sunset Crater to around 4 inches in Munds Park.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong area of low pressure from the Pacific Northwest brought heavy rain, very windy conditions, and high elevation snow on Friday December 7th through Saturday December 8th. The next portion of the storm system began to move across the state on late Sunday December 9th and lasted through Tuesday December 11th. This brought heavy snow to many areas along and north of the Mogollon Rim and the White Mountains.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain caused the East Verde River to rise. A Payson man was rescued after he tried to cross the River. His four wheel drive SUV was washed about 50 yards downstream where it was stopped by some boulders.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain fell across the lower elevations southern Apache County from December 7th through December 11th.  About 3.50 inches of rain and melted snow fell just north of McNary.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy snow fell over the White Mountains with 12 inches in McNary, 12 inches in Alpine, and 14 inches at Sunrise Ski Resort.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Portions of three counties experience flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rains fell at Queen Creek, with 1.61 inches at the Queen Creek landfill and 1.54 inches at East Maricopa Floodway and Queen Creek Road. High water forced road closures at Hunt and Ellsworth and the Magic Ranch subdivision. At least one car stalled in high water at Hunt Highway and Hawes.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Portions of three counties experience flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Roofing materials blown off several homes; mobile home damaged in White Rock, near San Carlos.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong thunderstorms and their associated winds caused very low visibility in many areas between Phoenix and Florence.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Near zero visibility reported in Tempe, Chandler and Queen Creek.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered showers and thunderstorms and the fast runoff resulted in flash flooding in parts of the Globe-Miami area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Spotter reported 2.75 inches of rain in less than one hour. Sheriffs office reported a swift-water rescue after many streets became flooded during the height of the storm.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong damaging winds and dense blowing dust hits Coolidge.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm winds caused roof damage and took down power lines in Coolidge.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms formed over the mountains south of Interstate 8 and moved toward the northeast.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Power and phones knocked out, tree limbs down, and 1.75 inches of rain in only 50 minutes.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Very strong winds and low visibility affected many desert areas late in the afternoon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Dust storm spread across portions of Pinal County, with near zero visibility at times. Numerous traffic accidents occurred at the time of the low visibility.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered thunderstorms pushed through parts of the East Valley, knocking down power lines.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Authorities in the Gila River Indian Community estimated winds as high as 80 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered thunderstorms formed over parts of Phoenix with locally strong winds at the airport.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning struck a home near 51st Ave and Indian School Road. No major damage was reported, but a small attic fire was quickly put out.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: South winds of 30 to 40 mph resulted in widespread areas of blowing dust.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Dense blowing dust reported in Florence.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Early morning thunderstorms generated areas of dense blowing dust which cut the visibility to less than 1/4 mile in Yuma.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Visibility reported to be less than 1/4 mile, due to blowing dust.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Dense blowing dust with low visibility spread throughout many East Valley communities.  In addition, thunderstorms brought gusty winds to near 60 mph in Apache Junction.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Numerous reports of visibility less than 1/4 mile across Mesa, Gilbert and Chandler.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Dense blowing dust with low visibility spread throughout many East Valley communities.  In addition, thunderstorms brought gusty winds to near 60 mph in Apache Junction.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A microburst struck a Chandler RV and trailer park, damaging at least one trailer and taking down power lines and uprooting trees.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms developed over the far East Valley resulting in damage to homes, power lines and trees.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Large trees down...including a 50 foot pine tree near Southern avenue and Greenfield road. Four homes damaged near Sossaman road and Main street. Power lines were down near Main street and Southern avenue.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Very heavy rain and flooding of low spots in parts of Southern Gila county.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Gila county sheriffs office reported roads closed due to high water near Tonto Basin.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong winds from nearby thunderstorms affected some communities near Gila Bend.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Between Buckeye and Gila Bend trees were uprooted...signs blown down and one roof blown off a shop. Two miles south of Cotton Center a power pole snapped because of the winds.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorm produced severe hail in Kendrick Park.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: One half to 3/4 inch diameter hail fell near White Buffalo Ranch on Highway  180 (mile post 236).

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A severe thunderstorm produced torrential rain, hail, and flooding in and around Lake Montezuma.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong and cold upper level low pressure moved into Southeast Arizona bringing with it rare accumulating snow to the lower desert elevations including the Tucson metro area.  This was the first significant snowfall in Tucson since January 30, 2002. The heaviest snow with this event fell in Southeast Pinal, Graham and Cochise Counties with numerous locations above 4000 feet experiencing six inches or more of snow.  This storm had a significant impact on Southeast Arizona causing many schools and highways to be closed.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Twenty Four inches of snow fell in Hannagan Meadow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A vigorous low pressure system moved slowly across southern Arizona, resulting in moderate to heavy rain to much of the deserts. A daily record rainfall of 1.23 inches was set at Phoenix Sky Harbor airport.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Moderate to heavy rain persisted over much of the Phoenix metro area during the afternoon and evening on Friday November 30th. Some of the heavier amounts recorded on the 30th included 2.44 inches in Carefree and 2.20 inches at McDowell Mountain Park in Scottsdale. Some roads were flooded in North Scottsdale and the high water in the Indian Bend wash forced the closure of Thomas Road. Water was reported to be 3-4 inches deep across Desert Hills Drive in the far North Phoenix area.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong thunderstorms caused high winds and excessive rainfall throughout the Tucson Metro area and a dust storm in Pinal County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Twelve power poles were knocked down due to strong winds along Houghton Road north of Valencia Road leaving 1400 customers without power.  There was also a 60 foot tree uprooted in Tucson due to a wet microburst.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Three feet rise on Hickiwan wash in one hour.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Three and a half foot rise on Sabino creek in one hour.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Ten foot rise on Santa Cruz River at Congress street in 2 hours.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The combination of a cut off low off the coast of Baja and a cold front from the northwest brought heavy rain to northern Arizona. The cut off low fed abundant subtropical moisture over Arizona and the cold front brought substantial lift to this moisture.  The result was nearly continuous precipitation for 36 hours in some locations along the Mogollon Rim and northern Arizona.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain fell over Coconino County through the day.  Some rainfall totals include: Flagstaff Airport: 1.38, Dry Park RAWS: 0.90, Grand Canyon Airport 1.00, Mormon Lake RAWS 2.54, Promontory RAWS 2.52.

The result was nearly continuous precipitation for 36 hours in some locations along the Mogollon Rim and northern Arizona.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain fell across Yavapai County through the day. Some rainfall amounts include: Prescott Airport 0.65, Cherry RAWS 1.58, Crown King RAWS 3.78, Sunset Point RAWS 2.25, and Humbug Creek RAWS 1.47.  One home flooded in Crown King and a motor home was washed down Agua Fria River after the driver tried to cross the swollen river. Oak Creek flooded an RV park in Page Springs and trapped a woman in her RV.  Verde Valley Fire Department safely preformed a swift water rescue.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Very cold air spread across Southeast California and Arizona during the weekend of January 14, 2007. The coldest 2 consecutive mornings since December of 1978 occurred at Phoenix Sky Harbor airport when the minimum temperature dropped to 29 degrees on January 14 and 15. Low temperatures were well below freezing in nearly all desert locations with temperatures into the teens at the coldest spots. Record lows on the 14th included 21 at Imperial CA, 25 at Blythe CA, and 25 at Yuma AZ. On the 15th, a record low of 23 degrees was set at Imperial CA. Other lows on the 15th included 18 degrees at Coolidge, 20 at East Mesa, 23 at Waddell and 26 at Scottsdale airport. Some effects of the freeze included burst pipes, damaged plants, and widespread losses to citrus crops. One nursery in Mesa reported a loss of 200,000 ficus trees. The citrus crops alone suffered losses estimated between $2 million and $4 million.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Record low temperatures included 25 at Yuma AZ.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong area of low pressure from the Pacific Northwest brought heavy rain, very windy conditions, and high elevation snow on Friday December 7th through Saturday December 8th. The next portion of the storm system began to move across the state on late Sunday December 9th and lasted through Tuesday December 11th. This brought heavy snow to many areas along and north of the Mogollon Rim and the White Mountains.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain fell across southern Navajo County from December 7th though the  11th. The snow level gradually lowered with the first part of the storm. The second half of the storm brought rapidly lowering snow levels.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A hard freeze with record low temperatures across the Tucson area caused many problems with frozen water pipes which subsequently burst and caused damage.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Hundreds of Tucson area residents encountered bursting water pipes due to a Hard Freeze.  Some Tucson Water facilities including well sites and pump stations had pipe breaks.  Another pipe breakage caused a cell phone system switch to be damaged.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A significant winter storm impacted the higher elevations of Southeast Arizona on December 10th through December 11th. This was a two part event with the heaviest snow on December 11th. For the first portion of the event, an upper level low north of the Baja spur was kicked east across Southeast Arizona overnight December 10th into 11th. Quickly on its heels another disturbance moved south from the Great Basin toward western Arizona and then gradually moved east across Southeast Arizona on December 11th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy snowfall in excess of 8 inches occurred above 6500 feet on Mount Graham in the Pinaleno Mountains.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A winter storm moving through Southeast Arizona caused high winds in the Catalina Mountains and downstream of the Huachuca mountains in Cochise County mainly impacting areas in and near Sierra Vista.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong winds off the Huachuca Mountains caused widespread damage to areas around Sierra Vista and Fort Huachuca. Damage in Sierra Vista included a roof blown off a home, a carport ripped off its foundation, numerous trees knocked down including one onto a vehicle. At Fort Huachcua downed trees snapped power lines and a portion of the Mountain View Golf Course clubhouse's roof was damaged. Also, an 80 foot tall communications tower near Hereford Road and Highway 92 was bent almost halfway.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong winds hit Doney park during the day with gusts between 40 and 45 MPH common.  The peak wind gust of 64 MPH hit at 4:10 PM. This was a localized event.

High temperatures in many of the deserts west of Tucson approached 105F.  NWS Tucson issued heat advisories advising the public of the unusually hot May temperatures.

The man was believed to have died within the last couple of days.|Border Patrol agents found on Monday, May 14th the decomposing body of a suspected illegal entrant west of Sasabe who was believed to be a 16 year old Chiapas boy.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The second major storm system in two weeks contributed to excessive runoff and high water over a period of several days in parts of southern Gila County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: About 500 people were stranded in their Tonto Basin homes because of flooded roads and adjacent areas around Tonto Creek. The largest rainfall amounts from this series of storms exceeded 3 inches in some spots. The river gauge at the Tonto Creek site near Roosevelt peaked at over 12 feet on the 8th. Rainfall continued into the week of December 10th, prolonging the runoff and keeping FR470 through Tonto Basin closed for several days. Globe received 1.90, and Miami had 2.72 inches from the 8th through the 12th.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The second major storm system in two weeks contributed to excessive runoff and high water over a period of several days in parts of southern Gila County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flood waters in the Tonto Creek caused road closures in the Tonto Basin area. Residents received assistance at the Tonto Basin Fire Department Annex on Elm Street.

Bystanders at nearby Phoenix International Raceway pulled four young people from the pickup. TV images showed the bystanders breaking out the windows, reaching inside the overturned pickup, and tossing the two young childrem to other rescuers.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong and slow moving thunderstorms caused excessive rainfall, flash flooding and wind damage across portions of Southeast Arizona.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong thunderstorms in Eastern and Central Pima County due to abundant monsoon moisture extensive flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: One person drowned in a car that got swept into Rodeo Wash near east Irvington Road.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong thunderstorms in Eastern and Central Pima County due to abundant monsoon moisture extensive flash flooding.

Six people were rescued at Alvernon Road and Interstate 10 in flood waters.  Isolated rainfall amounts of 2-3 inches occurred from Davis-Monthan Air Force Base to the South side of Tucson.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered thunderstorms caused strong winds and Flash Flooding across Eastern Pima County and the Tohono O'odham Nation. Outflow winds from these thunderstorms also caused a dust storm in Southeast Pinal County.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Several homes were flooded in the Comstock neighborhood in Benson due to flash flooding.  A swift water rescue also took place at Interstate 10 and the Pomerene Road exit in Benson.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong and slow moving thunderstorms with heavy rain and strong winds moved through portions of the Tucson area causing flash flooding and wind damage.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Power poles down near Golf Links and Prudence in Tucson.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A sharp cold front whipped through Arizona resulting in winds well over 40 mph and dense blowing dust with visibility less than a quarter mile. Some roof damage was also reported in Parker.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Wind gusts over 40 mph were widespread across the Phoenix area, with a peak gust of 54 mph reported in Fountain Hills.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Unusual heat for so early in the season, together with increased moisture, resulted in widespread light showers, very strong winds and areas of dense blowing dust and sand.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A major dust storm with visibility less than 1/4 mile in spots, along with winds of 40 to 50 mph, moved quickly across the Phoenix metropolitan area during the afternoon. Trees were knocked over, power outages were reported, and flight delays affected Sky Harbor airport.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A sharp cold front whipped through Arizona resulting in winds well over 40 mph and dense blowing dust with visibility less than a quarter mile. Some roof damage was also reported in Parker.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Winds damaged some roofs of buildings in Parker. Winds also knocked out power during the morning hours.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain resulted in flooded roads near Globe.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Water reported covering State Route 188 north of Lake Roosevelt and south of Punkin Center. Officers reported the road impassable.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Flash flooding reported in the Wickenburg area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Rapid rises in creeks and washes in Wickenburg. Vulture Mine road reported closed between Route 60 and 93, other roads in the area flooded.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Severe thunderstorms occurred across Eastern Pima County producing considerable wind damage and flash flooding on the northwest side of Tucson.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Roads closed in north Scottsdale. At least 6 water  rescues. Several automatic gauges reported between 1.5 and 2.0 inch per hour rainfall rates. Floodwaters caused $2 million of damage at Desert Sun Elementary School in North Scottsdale.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Severe thunderstorms occurred across Eastern Pima County producing considerable wind damage and flash flooding on the northwest side of Tucson.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Numerous trees were downed due to strong thunderstorm winds in the vicinity of Oracle road and Ina road. Largest tree was about 6 inch diameter and the others 4 to 5 inches in diameter.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flash flooding from thunderstorms in the City of Douglas caused damage to several city buildings and facilities.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: A suspected illegal immigrant was struck and injured by lightning while traveling through the desert of the Altar Valley near Sasabe. Three of his companions were also shocked when the bolt hit the man who suffered minor injuries.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: As monsoonal moisture was starting to enter Southeast Arizona, thunderstorms developed across Cochise county.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorm produced nickle sized hail in Young.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Abundant monsoonal moisture caused numerous thunderstorms across much of Southeast Arizona.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain from a thunderstorm produced a flash flood and mudslide across Copper Mine Road.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain from a thunderstorm produced a flash flood and mudslide on Copper Mind Road.

Power lines were also blown over at the intersections of Campbell Road and Roger Road, Stone Road and Roger Road and at Stone Road and Country Club Road.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain caused flash flooding in northern Gila and southern Navajo Counties.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A large area of thunderstorms caused flash flooding from heavy rain across  Navajo County from Cibecue to Snowflake.  Highway 277 near Snowflake was a lake and several homes were flooded in Snowflake. Cottonwood Wash was over its banks just west of Snowflake. Paper Mill Road was flooded in Snowflake. Highway 60 was close due to a flash flood induced rock slide. Washes were running very high in Cibecue with several roads closed.  Cibecue Wash was running 10 feet deep...it is normally less than a foot deep.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: A cinder block wall was knocked down at a residence in Safford due to strong winds. In addition, debris covered much of Highway 191 from just south of Safford to near Artesia.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered thunderstorms and moderate to heavy rainfall spread across many desert areas.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: About 1.5 inches of rain in less than an hour resulted in flooding in Wickenburg. Torrential rainfall rates, about 2 inches per hour, fell in Phoenix. A roof of a central Phoenix Safeway store caved in due to the rainfall. Phoenix Fire Department rescued a 12 year-old from a flooded area near 35th Avenue and Loop 101.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Widespread areas of dense blowing dust with visibility less than 1/4 mile.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Very strong winds kicked up widespread areas of dense blowing dust. Spotters reported less than 1/4 mile visibility.|

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Over 2,000 homes and businesses were left without power as winds tore down poles in Coolidge. Phone service was cut and many trees were uprooted in Coolidge. Additional damage was reported in other areas of the county.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Roofs blown off homes located in the eastern portions of the Gila river Indian Reservation.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain from a thunderstorm caused flash flooding on the south of Sedona.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain from a thunderstorm caused flooding in the Sedona area. Mud, boulders, and high water blocked Highway 179 south side of Sedona. Some vehicles were stalled in the high water.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Widespread areas of dense blowing dust with visibility less than 1/4 mile.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Damaging winds from thunderstorms took down power lines on west Martin Road and caused roof damage in portions of Coolidge.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: A swift water rescue took place on the Pantano Wash at Speedway Boulevard where three teenagers needed to be rescued from the wash.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Low water crossings were flooded at Watson Canal, Kelley Canal and north of the Gila River/Pima Bridge.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Runoff from excessive rainfall on July 19th caused washes to flow across the Tucson Metro area.  In addition, new thunderstorms developed later in the day across Cochise County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Excessive rainfall and low visibility contributed to a fatal accident on Highway 92 near Hereford.  The Cochise County emergency dispatch facility flooded along with Highway 80 at Davis road.  In this location two vehicles were stuck in high water.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Runoff from excessive rainfall on July 19th caused washes to flow across the Tucson Metro area.  In addition, new thunderstorms developed later in the day across Cochise County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A tin roof was blown off of a Carport at Santa Rosa Ranch on the Tohono O'odham Nation due to strong winds from thunderstorms.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Daytime heating in combination with a moist flow caused thunderstorms to develop across Southeast Arizona.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Two mobile homes were destroyed and a traditional home partially destroyed in the Ventana Section of the Tucson Foothills.  There were two uprooted trees at Grant Road and Kolb Road in Tucson and an estimated 60 mph gust due to thunderstorms in Tucson.  A roof also collapsed at a furniture store near 22nd street and Wilmot road. In addition, these thunderstorms knocked down about 20 power poles near Palo Verde Road and Irvington Road.  There were 18,000 customers without power in the Tucson area.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong and slow moving thunderstorms with heavy rain and strong winds moved through portions of the Tucson area causing flash flooding and wind damage.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A measured wind gust to 63 mph occurred at the Davis-Monthan Air Force Based Automated Surface Observing System.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rains first hit the northwest part of Maricopa County, then spread into the Metro Phoenix area. Many streets were flooded, trees downed and considerable property damage.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Trees and power lines were downed through parts of Gilbert. Streetlights were also reported to be down due to the winds. Utility poles and at least one large billboard in the East Valley were damaged by winds.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Power line downed by high winds in North Phoenix.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Power line downed by high winds. Winds gusted to 55 mph at Sky Harbor airport. While walking in his yard, a man touched the live wire and was electrocuted.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The fist week of July saw very hot temperatures across many desert areas. The high temperature in Phoenix on the 4th was 116 degrees. The remainder of the month was also hot, with an average monthly temperature of 95.8 degrees in Phoenix...which was the 7th warmest on record. State and local health officials reminded residents about the importance of taking precautions against the heat.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Maricopa County officials reported 32 confirmed deaths as either heat related or heat caused. Twenty four were male and 8 were female.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong and slow moving thunderstorms caused excessive rainfall, flash flooding and wind damage across portions of Southeast Arizona.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Over a dozen trees and at least two power lines were downed near Fort Lowell and Dodge Blvd.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Abundant monsoonal moisture caused numerous thunderstorms across much of Southeast Arizona.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Power lines were knocked down along Miracle Mile Strip in Tucson.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Very moist and unstable air resulted in widespread showers and thunderstorms across much of South-Central Arizona. Many roads and low spots became flooded by late afternoon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms and very heavy rainfall spread across much of the area. A spotter in Carefree reported 1.5 inches of rain in less than one hour...and many roads and low spots were flooded. A wastewater treatment plant and a park was damaged in Carefree. Flooding was reported in Queen Creek, where a water line was damaged from the flash flood and resulted in the closure of Higley Road. A large ground fissure resulted from flooding at Queen Creek and 195th Street, and near Happy Road south of San Tan Blvd. One horse was reported killed in this fissure. |

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Moist southerly tropical flow over Arizona due to an area of low pressure off the California coast caused thunderstorms and flash flooding across Southeast Arizona.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Local rainfall amounts of one to two inches fell in portions of the Tucson area causing flash flooding.  A swift water rescue took place on the Rillito River between Dodge and Alvernon.  Another swift water rescue took place on the Pantano Wash near Valencia and Houghton on the east side of Tucson.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong and slow moving thunderstorms caused excessive rainfall, flash flooding and wind damage across portions of Southeast Arizona.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A vehicle was stuck in Brawley wash at Manville Road and Avra Road.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rains first hit the northwest part of Maricopa County, then spread into the Metro Phoenix area. Many streets were flooded, trees downed and considerable property damage.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Locally heavy rains resulted in flooded streets and considerable water in washes and other low-lying areas. A car stalled at Country Club Drive underneath the Broadway Road bridge where about 2 feet of water had accumulated. Several water rescues were made when vehicles became stuck in flooded areas on Carefree highway.|

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong thunderstorms in Eastern and Central Pima County due to abundant monsoon moisture extensive flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained spotter reported a wind gust estimate of 58 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong area of low pressure from the Pacific Northwest brought heavy rain, very windy conditions, and high elevation snow on Friday December 7th through Saturday December 8th. The next portion of the storm system began to move across the state on late Sunday December 9th and lasted through Tuesday December 11th. This brought heavy snow to many areas along and north of the Mogollon Rim and the White Mountains.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Seventeen inches of snow fell at the Flagstaff Airport.  Twenty-six inches of snow fell near Blue Ridge. Over 200 traffic accidents and slide offs were reported by law enforcement departments.  Nine of those involved minor injuries and there was one fatality.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong area of low pressure from the Pacific Northwest brought heavy rain, very windy conditions, and high elevation snow on Friday December 7th through Saturday December 8th. The next portion of the storm system began to move across the state on late Sunday December 9th and lasted through Tuesday December 11th. This brought heavy snow to many areas along and north of the Mogollon Rim and the White Mountains.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Twenty-two inches of snow fell at Forest Lakes.  Pinetop received 14 inches of snow.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Two snow and grapple showers caused several traffic accidents on Highway 89.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Two heavy snow and grapple showers impacted Highway 69 between Prescott Valley and Cordes Junction on December 11th. The first shower caused a three vehicles collision and five other vehicles slid off the highway. One person was flown to a hospital. The highway was closed for about 45 minutes. The second shower covered a one half mile stretch of Highway 69  causing one vehicle to cross over into oncoming traffic. The occupants of the car were cut out and flown to hospitals with life threatening |injuries.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Severe thunderstorms occurred across Eastern Pima County producing considerable wind damage and flash flooding on the northwest side of Tucson.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A swift water rescue occurred at River road and Swan road due to flash flooding.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Winds over 50 mph struck parts of Maricopa and southern Gila county on Thursday.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The strongest winds were measured at a remote sensor on top of South Mountain.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Winds over 50 mph struck parts of Maricopa and southern Gila county on Thursday.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Hail as large as 3/4 inch in diameter was reported by a spotter.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain caused flash flooding in northern Gila and southern Navajo Counties.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flash flooding in Star Valley flooded about 20 homes and caused debris/rock slides in many locations. All crossings of the East Verde River were closed...one crossing was flooded 50 yards wide and up to 10 feet deep. Four homes were evacuated in Beaver Valley. New asphalt was ripped off parts of the road in Beaver Valley. Flooding was also reported on Highway 73 from near Carrizo to near White River.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Monsoonal moisture produced severe thunderstorms with wind damage on the Northwest side of Tucson and east of Ajo in Western Pima County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: An off duty National Weather Service employee reported several large tree limbs broken near Ina Road and Mona Lisa Road.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain caused widespread flash flooding in the Chinle Valley.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Widespread flooding was reported in Chinle by Navajo County Sheriff's office. Flooding was also reported along Highway 59 near Rough Rock. Businesses and some homes along Highway 91 in Chinle were under up to two feet of water.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm winds caused considerable damage to the Las Casitas trailer park south of town, and power lines and poles down in town.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: About 90 mobile homes damaged or destroyed at Las Casitas trailer park. One third of them were blown off their foundations. About 150 people evacuated due to damage and numerous gas leaks. Unknown number of people had minor injuries. Numerous trees were blown down and about a mile-long stretch of power poles were damaged.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms caused power outages and flash flooding in the Holbrook area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain flooded several streets in Holbrook.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms caused power outages and flash flooding in the Holbrook area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning knocked down power lines and caused power outages in Holbrook.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain caused flash flooding across Highway 191 south of Sanders.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain from thunderstorms caused flash flooding across Highway 191 between mile post 346 to 347 about 20 miles south-southeast of Sanders.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The average daily high temperature in Phoenix was 106 degrees with daily maximum or minimum records set on 11 days in August. This was also the warmest summer on record, with an average temperature of 94.9F for June, July and August. Also there were 32 days through the summer where the high temperature was at or above 110 degrees.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: August temperatures continued much above normal, making 32 days during the summer where temperatures equalled or exceeded 110 degrees.|

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong thunderstorms produced heavy rainfall over the higher terrain of the Catalina Mountains. This heavy rainfall produced a flash flood which moved downstream into Bear Canyon and the popular Seven Falls area causing two fatalities. Flash flooding also occurred in the Whetstone area of Cochise County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flash flooding occurred in Whetstone at an RV dealership mainly impacting the maintenance bays.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered thunderstorms across Southeast Arizona caused hail and wind damage in Tucson and flash flooding in Nogales.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Three quarter inch hail was reported near Vail from a trained spotter.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered thunderstorms across Southeast Arizona caused hail and wind damage in Tucson and flash flooding in Nogales.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Portions of downtown Nogales experienced flash flooding. Extensive damage occurred to the Nogales Wash, which is a concrete wash that flows through the city of Nogales. City officials estimated damage at $10 million on the U.S. side of the border.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Severe thunderstorms occurred across Eastern Pima County producing considerable wind damage and flash flooding on the northwest side of Tucson.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Penny size hail was reported by a trained spotter.

EVENT_NARRATIVE: Power lines were reported down at Oracle road and River road.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A severe thunderstorm caused extensive damage to a neighborhood near Eloy in Pinal County. Meanwhile, southwest of Tucson, lightning struck a suspected illegal immigrant in the Altar Valley.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A microburst caused considerable damage near Arizona City, partially destroying structures and roofs, overturning cars and knocking down walls.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain caused flash flooding west of Snowflake.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain from a thunderstorm caused flash flooding west of mile marker 318 on State Road 277 and Pulp Mill Road.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain caused flash flooding in Christiopher Creek.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flash flooding was reported in Christopher Creek from the edge of the Mogollon Rim to about 10 miles downstream from Highway 260 near the community of Christopher Creek.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A severe thunderstorm produced torrential rain, hail, and flooding in and around Lake Montezuma.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Wet Beaver Creek at Montezuma Castle National Monument rose 8 feet in one hour. Three to 4 inches of rain fell in one hour. Large sections of the bank along Wet Beaver Creek eroded away. Russell Wash flowed out if its banks with railroad ties floating down the street. Ten to twenty homes have water in them. Two bridges in the town of Lake Montezuma were damaged. Three vehicles were picked and carred down a street by floodwater.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain caused flash flooding in Yavapai County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flash flooding closed the intersection of 6th and Date Streets.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain caused flash flooding in Yavapai County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Water was running over roads in Cornville.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain caused flash flooding in Yavapai County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Highway 97 between Highways 96 & 93 south of Bagdad was impassable due to flooding.  The road washed out.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Over 2,000 homes and businesses were left without power as winds tore down poles in Coolidge. Phone service was cut and many trees were uprooted in Coolidge. Additional damage was reported in other areas of the county.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: About 11 power poles were destroyed along the west side of Arizona Boulevard on the edge of the Casa Grande Ruins National Monument in Coolidge. Power was out for about 2300 households and businesses for more than 40 hours. Phone service was disrupted. Winds also uprooted trees in the area.|The Red Cross estimated that more than 340 people had received assistance in the form of food, water and shelter since a cooling station was established at the high school.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong thunderstorms produced heavy rainfall over the higher terrain of the Catalina Mountains. This heavy rainfall produced a flash flood which moved downstream into Bear Canyon and the popular Seven Falls area causing two fatalities. Flash flooding also occurred in the Whetstone area of Cochise County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Large wall of water moved down seven falls in Bear Canyon. About 50 people sought higher ground and many of those needed to be rescued by helicopter.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain caused flash flooding in St Johns.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Run off from heavy rain (over three inches) covered numerous roads in St Johns. One and a half to two feet of water covered on intersection. At least one house was flooded.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A winter storm moving through Southeast Arizona caused high winds in the Catalina Mountains and downstream of the Huachuca mountains in Cochise County mainly impacting areas in and near Sierra Vista.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong winds on Mt. Lemmon and Summerhaven late November 30th into the early morning hours of December 1st caused considerable damage. Hundreds of trees were knocked down, three cabin roofs were destroyed and many power lines were knocked down. A mesowest anemometer recorded wind gusts to about 84mph before being knocked out. Damage estimate of $100,000 was included in the November summary.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper level low stalled west of Baja for several days bringing northward ample subtropical moisture. On November 30th the upper low pushed east toward Arizona brining heavy rainfall and strong winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong winds on Mt. Lemmon and Summerhaven late November 30th into the early morning hours of December 1st caused considerable damage. Hundreds of trees were knocked down, three cabin roofs were destroyed and many power lines were knocked down. A mesowest anemometer recorded wind gusts to about 84mph before being knocked out.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms resulted in considerable damage in portions of Yuma after winds gusted to 84 mph at the Yuma Airport.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Numerous trees and as many as 11 power poles reported down due to strong winds. Peak gusts to 84 mph were recorded at the Yuma airport. Arizona Public Service reported about 9,600 people were left without power Sunday morning. Yuma Police responded to more than 120 emergency calls for service, most of which were storm related. Numerous eyewitnesses described the area around the 100 block of West 27th Place as the worst-hit section of town. Condos in that area had considerable roof damage with ceilings collapsing onto living rooms and dining rooms. Large hail and localized flooding was also reported in Yuma.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Flooding was reported along Campaign Creek between Globe and Roosevelt.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flash flooding reported in Roosevelt Estates. Campaign creek had one to two feet of water approximately 60 feet wide that flowed across Mesquite Street.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain caused a rock slide on Highway 60 and flash flooding on State Route 73.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain caused a rock slide that close Highway 60 at mile marker 321. Flash flooding was observed on State Route 73. Huston Creek overflowed in Star Valley and flooded about 20 homes and/or trailers.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A funnel cloud was reported north of Winslow.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A funnel cloud or possible tornado was reported in the area northwest to north of Winslow from 400 PM MST to 425 PM MST.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A severe thunderstorm produced one inch diameter hail in Chino Valley.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: One inch diameter hail was reported in Chino Valley. Wind gusts to 55 MPH and downed tree branches were also reported.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Locally heavy rain hit portions of South-central Arizona during the late afternoon hours.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Locally heavy rain was reported to be a factor in the series of vehicle accidents on US 60. The most serious wreck was a 27-car pileup in the westbound lanes between Rural and McClintock Drive.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Monsoonal moisture produced severe thunderstorms with wind damage on the Northwest side of Tucson and east of Ajo in Western Pima County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Power lines were downed east of Ajo causing a complete power outage across Western Pima County.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Locally heavy rains fell in portions of Southern Gila County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Highway 60 reported flooded with rock slides in the area. Spotter to the south of Globe reported .50 inch in only 20 minutes.|

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorm produced flash flooding about one mile north of Holbrook on Highway 77.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A thunderstorm with heavy rain produced flash flooding over Highway 77 about one mile north of Holbrook.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Slow moving storms affected parts of southern Gila County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flooding reported in portions of Miami and Globe.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Dense blowing dust with low visibility spread throughout many East Valley communities.  In addition, thunderstorms brought gusty winds to near 60 mph in Apache Junction.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Strong winds caused power lines to come down in Apache Junction.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Dense blowing dust with low visibility spread throughout many East Valley communities.  In addition, thunderstorms brought gusty winds to near 60 mph in Apache Junction.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Winds estimated as high as 60 mph blew a roof off a wood structure near Route 60 and Goldfield Road.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms resulted in considerable damage in portions of Yuma after winds gusted to 84 mph at the Yuma Airport.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Large hail was reported by the public in Yuma.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Large hail reported in parts of Scottsdale.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Large hail was reported by the public.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm winds caused considerable damage to the Las Casitas trailer park south of town, and power lines and poles down in town.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Two or more railroad crossing arms along Jimmy Kerr Blvd. were damaged due to strong winds.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm winds caused considerable damage to the Las Casitas trailer park south of town, and power lines and poles down in town.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Power lines and poles along Arizola Ave were damaged by high winds.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm winds caused considerable damage to the Las Casitas trailer park south of town, and power lines and poles down in town.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Power poles were blown down on Thornton Road just north of Interstate 8.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered thunderstorms formed over parts of Phoenix with locally strong winds at the airport.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Winds gusted to 59 mph at Sky Harbor airport.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rainfall resulted in local flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Sols wash was reported flooded and water was flowing over highway 93.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Very moist and unstable air resulted in widespread showers and thunderstorms across much of South-Central Arizona. Many roads and low spots became flooded by late afternoon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Winds gusted to 60 mph and blew down several power lines.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper level low stalled west of Baja for several days bringing northward ample subtropical moisture. On November 30th the upper low pushed east toward Arizona brining heavy rainfall and strong winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Twenty illegal entrants were rescued after being trapped by floodwaters on an embankment 20 miles south of Three Points. In addition, the area near milepost 25 on Arizona 286 became impassable.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper level low stalled west of Baja for several days bringing northward ample subtropical moisture. On November 30th the upper low pushed east toward Arizona brining heavy rainfall and strong winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Catalina highway experienced flooding due to heavy rainfall causing rock slides along the road.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Very moist and unstable air resulted in widespread showers and thunderstorms across much of South-Central Arizona. Many roads and low spots became flooded by late afternoon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Winds as high as 70 mph damaged at least one power pole.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Portions of three counties experience flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flooding was reported by a COOP observer in Roosevelt.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Portions of three counties experience flash flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Rock slides were reported on SR 60 between the Queen Creek tunnel and Devils Canyon.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered showers and thunderstorms and the fast runoff resulted in flash flooding in parts of the Globe-Miami area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: At least one swift-water rescue was reported after streets and roads became flooded.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Visibility reduced to near zero in spots...after thunderstorms produced strong and gusty winds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Near zero visibility reported near highway 347 and Maricopa Road.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong damaging winds and dense blowing dust hits Coolidge.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Visibility less than 100 yards was reported across much of Northwest Pinal County.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The combination of a cut off low off the coast of Baja and a cold front from the northwest brought heavy rain to northern Arizona. The cut off low fed abundant subtropical moisture over Arizona and the cold front brought substantial lift to this moisture.  The result was nearly continuous precipitation for 36 hours in some locations along the Mogollon Rim and northern Arizona.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain fell over Apache county during the day. Some rainfall totals include: Pine Hill (RAWS) 0.80,  Window Rock Airport 0.65, St Johns Airport 0.40, Alpine. 1.80, and Greer 0.90.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Afternoon thunderstorms and very heavy rainfall resulted in flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Torrential rain resulted in flooded roads and streets near Mohawk canal.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A push of monsoon moisture brought thunderstorms with flash flooding and severe weather to the Mojave Desert and Southern Great Basin from July 24th through the 30th.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Two roads closed in Golden Valley due to flooding.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Very strong winds and low visibility affected many desert areas late in the afternoon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm winds blew out windows at the Superior administration building, and damaged a nearby truck window. Trees were downed by strong winds.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A weak mid level disturbance combined with record heat caused a storm which produced a microburst.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A microburst brought down seven power poles in Tucson along Irvington Road between Camino Seco and Harrison leaving about 2675 structures without power.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorm wind gusts in Yavapai County caused damage.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A power pole was blown down in a dry microburst north of Dewey. About 2,000 customers were without power for about two hours.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper level low tracking across Southeast Arizona caused thunderstorms across portions of the Tucson area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A spotter estimated a wind gust of 60 mph and reported small branches down in Catalina.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain from a thunderstorm caused flash flooding in Chevlon Ranch.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rain from a thunderstrom caused flooding the Chevlon Ranch area, about 20 miles north of Heber.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Evening thunderstorms caused strong winds across portions of Graham and Greenlee County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A strong thunderstorm knocked down two power poles in Safford.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Ample monsoonal moisture combined with daytime heating caused strong thunderstorms over Santa Cruz County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Several trees were knocked down in the downtown area of Nogales due to strong winds from a strong thunderstorm.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A funnel cloud was reported near Rock Point.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A funnel cloud was reported near Rock Point north of Highway 191.
